title: Self-align via structure by selective deposition
abstract: In one embodiment, a self-aligned via is presented. In one embodiment, an inhibitor layer is selectively deposited on the lower conductive region. In one embodiment, a dielectric is selectively deposited on the lower conductive region. In one embodiment, the deposited dielectric may be selectively etched. In one embodiment, an inhibitor is selectively deposited on the lower dielectric region. In one embodiment, a dielectric is selectively deposited on the lower dielectric region. In one embodiment, the deposited dielectric over the lower conductive region has a different etch rate than the deposited dielectric over the lower dielectric region which may lead to a via structure that is aligned with the lower conductive region.
claims: 1. A semiconductor structure, comprising: a first dielectric layer disposed over a substrate; a first conductor and a second conductor disposed in the first dielectric layer, top surfaces of the first conductor, the second conductor, and the first dielectric layer are horizontally aligned; a second dielectric layer disposed over the top surface of the first conductor, wherein sidewalls of the second dielectric layer are vertically aligned with sidewalls of the first conductor; a third dielectric layer disposed adjacent the second dielectric layer and interposed between the first conductor and the second conductor, wherein the third dielectric layer and the second dielectric layer have different compositions; a via coupled to the second conductor, wherein a first sidewall of the via is vertically aligned with a first sidewall of the second conductor and a second sidewall of the via is separated from a sidewall of the third dielectric layer; and a fourth dielectric layer interposed between the second sidewall of the via and the sidewall of the third dielectric layer, wherein the fourth dielectric layer and the second dielectric layer have the same composition.
